<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }


    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') == -1){
        // Hold events if cookie wall is not accepted and no decision was made yet
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "holdEvents"
        });
    }
</script>

<script src="https://cdn.optimizely.com/js/5530288147660800.js"></script>

<script>
    // Check if cookie wall is accepted sufficiently
    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well
    window.addEventListener('OneTrustGroupsUpdated', (event) => {
        if (event.detail.includes('C0002')) {
            window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
            window.optimizely.push({
                "type": "sendEvents"
            });
        } else {
            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined
            // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
            window["optimizely"].push({
                "type": "disable",
                "scope": "tracking"
            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;

  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }

        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",

        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted

            "type": "sendEvents"
        });

            window.optimizely.push({

            });

            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined

    });

  min-width: 0;
  width: 100% !important;
  max-width: 800px !important;
  top: 50% !important;
  transform: translate(0, -50%);
  border-radius: 4px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

    <article class="article" data-article-id="4509799" data-video-id="" data-widget="article/article" data-initialised="true">

        <div class="article__wrapper">


            <div class="article__content js-article__content" data-flatplan-body="">
                <p>See below for all the deals announced by Premier League clubs during the January 2026 transfer window, which closed at 19:00 GMT on Monday 2 February.</p>

<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }


    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') == -1){

            "type": "holdEvents"
        });
    }
</script>

    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well

            });

            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined

            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

                <p class="article__subtitle">Feature</p>

                <p class="article__summary">Adrian Clarke explains how Arsenal teenager's attributes are a good fit for No 6 or No 8 role</p>

            <div class="article__content js-article__content" data-flatplan-body="">
                <p><em>After Myles Lewis-Skelly signs a new contract with Arsenal, football writer Adrian Clarke looks at what makes the teenage Gunner such an exciting talent, and what we can expect to see from him in 2025/26.</em></p>
<p>Myles Lewis-Skelly only made his full Premier League debut in mid-December, yet no one is surprised at his nomination for the PFA Young Player of the Year award.</p>
<p>That shows how much startling progress the 18-year-old has made in 2025.</p>
<p>He has excelled as an inverted left-back for <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/1/Arsenal/overview">Arsenal</a> to such a degree that his intelligent, forceful approach is now an integral part of <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/managers/53055/Mikel-Arteta/overview">Mikel Arteta</a>’s style of play.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">

<section data-non-embeddable-d2c="" class="article-embeddable ">

        <h4 class="article-embeddable__title">
            Myles Lewis-Skelly: 'I want a legacy here'
	<svg class="icon icn" aria-hidden="true">
		<use xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="https://web.premierleague.pulselive.com/resources/rebrand/v7.153.79/i/svg-output/icons.svg#icn-external-no-fill"></use>
	</svg>
        </h4>
        <p class="article-embeddable__description u-hide-mob"></p>
    </a>
</section></div><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4326369" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="Myles Lewis-Skelly: 'I want a legacy here' Content Card" href="https://www.arsenal.com/news/myles-lewis-skelly-i-want-legacy-here" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback "><div class="content-card__image"><div class="js-lazy-load u-observed lazy-image-wrapper " style=""><picture class="media-thumbnail__image object-fit-cover-picture"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2025/06/26/a2c462eb-5c98-4f8b-b98f-0f5b8cc5b58e/lewis-skelly-contract_yicbgra7.jpg?height=186&width=332" alt="Myles Lewis-Skelly signing a new contract with Arsenal" loading="lazy" class="img undefined picture__img object-fit-cover-picture__img" width="332" height="186"></picture></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">Myles Lewis-Skelly: 'I want a legacy here'</p></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><h5>Playing out of position</h5>
<p>What makes <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/players/114564/Myles-Lewis-Skelly/stats">Lewis-Skelly</a>’s sudden rise to prominence extra special, is the fact he is not a natural left-back.</p>
<p>He played as a central defender for his school team, but at Arsenal's Hale End academy he was always regarded as a dynamic, ball-carrying central midfielder.</p>
<p>As recently as January 2024, Lewis-Skelly spoke about his preferred position on the <a href="https://www.arsenal.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">official Arsenal website</a>, and there was no reference at all to playing on the left side.</p>
<p>Still an Under-18s star at that point, he told the club channel, "My best position is either a No 6 or No 8. I’m not too sure yet, but I’m just continuing to try my best to master both. Whatever it takes, I’m ready to play in the [No] 6 or [No] 8 role.</p>
<p>"Every time I look at <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/players/15202/Declan-Rice/stats">Declan Rice</a>, I’m watching him and looking to see how he’s at the top of his game. What he’s good at, I can use it in my game and try to reach the top."</p>
<p>After that interview was published Arsenal began to experiment with Lewis-Skelly as an inverted left-back late on in the 2023/24 campaign for their U18s and Under-21s sides.</p>
<p>This was in preparation for him being used in that position at senior level, as manager Arteta explained earlier this year.</p>
<p>"The moment that I saw him, the only way I could think was to change his position," said Arteta.</p>
<p>"I spoke to him and said I think this is where you’re going to have it [a first-team opportunity], because I think it fits a lot of his qualities, the way we play, and it fits because we had necessities in the position. So, we started to work on that."</p>
<p>An injury crisis at left-back last autumn left Arteta short of options, and ever since Lewis-Skelly's debut as a late sub at <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/11/Manchester-City/overview">Manchester City</a> on 22 September, that is where Arsenal supporters have seen him perform.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">

</div><h5>The back story</h5>
<p>Lewis-Skelly was born in Denmark Hill in south London, but his family moved north of the river early in his childhood.</p>
<p>He was a gifted tennis player with potential, but his talent as a young footballer quickly caught eye of several scouts, and after trials with <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/4/Chelsea/overview">Chelsea</a> and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/21/Tottenham-Hotspur/overview">Tottenham Hotspur</a>, he chose to join Arsenal at the age of eight.</p>
<p>In the same intake was <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/players/114566/Ethan-Nwaneri/stats">Ethan Nwaneri</a>, and the pair grew up together at the Hale End academy, becoming close friends, pushing one another to improve.</p>
<p>He revelled in Adam Birchall’s sides (current head coach of Arsenal U18s) at Under-13s, Under-14s and Under-15s level, elevating his status to a player of great potential.</p>
<p>Then under Jack Wilshere, Lewis-Skelly was a part of the Arsenal U18s side that reached the 2022/23 FA Youth Cup final. Only 16 at the time, he scored a last-minute semi-final winner against Man City at the Emirates Stadium to send the young Gunners into the final, which they lost to <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/25/West-Ham-United/overview">West Ham United</a>.</p>
<p>He has been part of the England set-up since he was 14, representing his country at the 2023 Under-17s Euros and the 2023 U17s World Cup in Indonesia.</p>
<p>And in March this year he made his senior England debut in a World Cup qualifier against Albania.</p>
<p>Ahead of that match, England head coach Thomas Tuchel said of Lewis-Skelly: "I understand why everyone loves the boy – a big character and very talented player. He is ready to go."</p>
<p>Lewis-Skelly proved that point in emphatic style, scoring a 20th-minute opener in a 2-0 win to become the youngest England debutant to score for his country, aged just 18 years and 176 days.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">

        <h4 class="article-embeddable__title">
            What Lewis-Skelly's team-mates say about him
	<svg class="icon icn" aria-hidden="true">

</section></div><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4326521" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="What Lewis-Skelly's team-mates say about him Content Card" href="https://www.arsenal.com/news/myles-lewis-skelly-told-his-teammates" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback content-card__thumbnail--t3 content-card__thumbnail--club-badge"><div class="content-card__image"><div data-testid="clubBadge" class="club-badge club-badge--x-large"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.com/premierleague25/badges-alt/3.svg" alt=" club badge" loading="lazy" class="club-badge__img"></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">What Lewis-Skelly's team-mates say about him</p></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><h5>What makes him so special?</h5>
<p>Lewis-Skelly uses his body remarkably well, and this makes him incredibly hard to knock off the ball.</p>
<p>The Arsenal youngster can dominate opposition wingers, or any central midfielders he encounters infield, and is fearless, determined and strong beyond his years.</p>
<p>Remarkably, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/15/Nottingham-Forest/overview">Nottingham Forest</a>’s towering central defender <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/players/16035/Nikola-Milenkovic/stats">Nikola Milenkovic</a> was the only Premier League outfielder to boast more success in his duels than Lewis-Skelly last season.</p>
<p>It is borderline ridiculous for a teenager to be near the top of this list at all.</p>
<h6>Most successful duels won 24/25*</h6>

<thead>

</tr>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
<p><em>*Minimum 15 starts</em></p>
<p>He is not just strong, but also technically outstanding.</p>
<p>Lewis-Skelly is able to stay calm under pressure, is press-resistant, and with Arteta trusting him to receive the ball in tight areas, he is regularly tested when Arsenal hold possession.</p>
<p>He rarely misplaces a pass, keeps his distribution short and simple, and is not afraid to move it through the lines either.</p>
<p>Lewis-Skelly's pass accuracy numbers are phenomenal for someone with such limited experience. </p>
<h6>Highest passing accuracy 24/25*</h6>

<p><em>*Minimum 15 starts</em></p>
<p>As you can see on this distribution map (below) he frequently comes into central midfield to help the Gunners in the build-up phase.</p>
<p>In central areas he barely gave the ball away all season.</p>
<h6>Lewis-Skelly 24/25 pass map</h6><div class="articleWidget full-width">



    <figure class="embeddable-photo " data-non-embeddable-d2c="">

</div><div class="articleWidget full-width">

    </div>


</div><p>Lewis-Skelly is not the kind of left-back who flies around the outside to deliver crosses on the overlap from advanced areas.</p>
<p>Instead, he likes to link play with others and drive forward with the ball at his feet.</p>
<p>Using his natural power to great effect, he regularly made long, marauding dribbles into the opposition half last season.</p>
<p>Lewis-Skelly is the division’s best defender when it comes to making progressive ball carries and rides challenges for fun.</p>
<h6>Most progressive ball carries by defender 24/25</h6>

</tr>
<tr style="height: 16.7969px;">

<tr style="height: 16.7969px;">

</tr>

<td style="width: 54.5303%; text-align: center; height: 16.7969px;">12.5m</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
<p>Rival players consistently got frustrated having to deal with the teenage talent in 2024/25.</p>
<p>Finding it hard to snatch the ball away from him, Lewis-Skelly was fouled 3.09 times per 90 minutes in his debut campaign.</p>
<p>Only two Premier League players won more free-kicks on average.</p>
<p>In so many ways, the youngster provides added benefits to Arsenal’s starting XI.</p>
<h6>Most fouls won per 90 mins 24/25*</h6>

<p><em>*Minimum 15 starts</em></p>
<h5>Highlight so far</h5>
<p>During an unbelievable 2025 for Lewis-Skelly, featuring a string of outstanding performances, his first Arsenal goal stands out as the highlight at club level.</p>
<p>Dancing into the box before firing a powerful shot into the corner, his strike against Man City in a 5-1 win – and subsequent celebration – made headline news.</p>
<p>He is sure to score plenty more for the Gunners.</p>
<h5>A grounded young star</h5>
<p>Lewis-Skelly’s future is likely to be inside Arsenal’s midfield, and we could see him used there more often in 2025/26.</p>
<p>His composure on the ball, neat passing, physicality and ball-carrying skills make him ideally suited to operate in either the No 6 or No 8 roles he imagined himself playing in.</p>
<p>For now though, he seems very happy to develop his game as a left-back and there will be no complaints from him about playing in that position.</p>
<p>Lewis-Skelly is grounded and has been studying Spanish and Business Studies at AS Level in his spare time.</p>
<p>He also supports his mum, Marcia, with the No 1 Fan Club she launched in 2022 - an initiative aimed at providing support, education and a community for parents whose children are involved at football academies.</p>
<p>On and off the pitch, Lewis-Skelly is a talented, likeable individual with an exceptionally bright future ahead of him.</p>

gold_text:
Feature

Adrian Clarke explains how Arsenal teenager's attributes are a good fit for No 6 or No 8 role

After Myles Lewis-Skelly signs a new contract with Arsenal, football writer Adrian Clarke looks at what makes the teenage Gunner such an exciting talent, and what we can expect to see from him in 2025/26.

Myles Lewis-Skelly only made his full Premier League debut in mid-December, yet no one is surprised at his nomination for the PFA Young Player of the Year award.

That shows how much startling progress the 18-year-old has made in 2025.

He has excelled as an inverted left-back for Arsenal to such a degree that his intelligent, forceful approach is now an integral part of Mikel Arteta’s style of play.
Playing out of position
What makes Lewis-Skelly’s sudden rise to prominence extra special, is the fact he is not a natural left-back.

He played as a central defender for his school team, but at Arsenal's Hale End academy he was always regarded as a dynamic, ball-carrying central midfielder.

As recently as January 2024, Lewis-Skelly spoke about his preferred position on the official Arsenal website, and there was no reference at all to playing on the left side.

Still an Under-18s star at that point, he told the club channel, "My best position is either a No 6 or No 8. I’m not too sure yet, but I’m just continuing to try my best to master both. Whatever it takes, I’m ready to play in the [No] 6 or [No] 8 role.

"Every time I look at Declan Rice, I’m watching him and looking to see how he’s at the top of his game. What he’s good at, I can use it in my game and try to reach the top."

After that interview was published Arsenal began to experiment with Lewis-Skelly as an inverted left-back late on in the 2023/24 campaign for their U18s and Under-21s sides.

This was in preparation for him being used in that position at senior level, as manager Arteta explained earlier this year.

"The moment that I saw him, the only way I could think was to change his position," said Arteta.

"I spoke to him and said I think this is where you’re going to have it [a first-team opportunity], because I think it fits a lot of his qualities, the way we play, and it fits because we had necessities in the position. So, we started to work on that."

An injury crisis at left-back last autumn left Arteta short of options, and ever since Lewis-Skelly's debut as a late sub at Manchester City on 22 September, that is where Arsenal supporters have seen him perform.
The back story
Lewis-Skelly was born in Denmark Hill in south London, but his family moved north of the river early in his childhood.

He was a gifted tennis player with potential, but his talent as a young footballer quickly caught eye of several scouts, and after trials with Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur, he chose to join Arsenal at the age of eight.

In the same intake was Ethan Nwaneri, and the pair grew up together at the Hale End academy, becoming close friends, pushing one another to improve.

He revelled in Adam Birchall’s sides (current head coach of Arsenal U18s) at Under-13s, Under-14s and Under-15s level, elevating his status to a player of great potential.

Then under Jack Wilshere, Lewis-Skelly was a part of the Arsenal U18s side that reached the 2022/23 FA Youth Cup final. Only 16 at the time, he scored a last-minute semi-final winner against Man City at the Emirates Stadium to send the young Gunners into the final, which they lost to West Ham United.

He has been part of the England set-up since he was 14, representing his country at the 2023 Under-17s Euros and the 2023 U17s World Cup in Indonesia.

And in March this year he made his senior England debut in a World Cup qualifier against Albania.

Ahead of that match, England head coach Thomas Tuchel said of Lewis-Skelly: "I understand why everyone loves the boy – a big character and very talented player. He is ready to go."

Lewis-Skelly proved that point in emphatic style, scoring a 20th-minute opener in a 2-0 win to become the youngest England debutant to score for his country, aged just 18 years and 176 days.
What makes him so special?
Lewis-Skelly uses his body remarkably well, and this makes him incredibly hard to knock off the ball.

The Arsenal youngster can dominate opposition wingers, or any central midfielders he encounters infield, and is fearless, determined and strong beyond his years.

Remarkably, Nottingham Forest’s towering central defender Nikola Milenkovic was the only Premier League outfielder to boast more success in his duels than Lewis-Skelly last season.

It is borderline ridiculous for a teenager to be near the top of this list at all.

Most successful duels won 24/25*
Player	Duel success %
Nikola Milenkovic 	70.22
Myles Lewis-Skelly	69.70
Ezro Konsa	68.97
Virgil van Dijk	68.65
Cameron Burgess	68.42
*Minimum 15 starts

He is not just strong, but also technically outstanding.

Lewis-Skelly is able to stay calm under pressure, is press-resistant, and with Arteta trusting him to receive the ball in tight areas, he is regularly tested when Arsenal hold possession.

He rarely misplaces a pass, keeps his distribution short and simple, and is not afraid to move it through the lines either.

Lewis-Skelly's pass accuracy numbers are phenomenal for someone with such limited experience. 

Highest passing accuracy 24/25*
Player	Passing accuracy %
William Saliba	94.58%
Manuel Akanji	94.08%
Ruben Dias	93.76%
Curtis Jones	93.67%
Myles Lewis-Skelly	93.55%
*Minimum 15 starts

As you can see on this distribution map (below) he frequently comes into central midfield to help the Gunners in the build-up phase.

In central areas he barely gave the ball away all season.
Lewis-Skelly is not the kind of left-back who flies around the outside to deliver crosses on the overlap from advanced areas.

Instead, he likes to link play with others and drive forward with the ball at his feet.

Using his natural power to great effect, he regularly made long, marauding dribbles into the opposition half last season.

Lewis-Skelly is the division’s best defender when it comes to making progressive ball carries and rides challenges for fun.

Most progressive ball carries by defender 24/25
Player	Avg. progressive ball carry
Myles Lewis-Skelly	12.9m
Destiny Udogie	12.7m
Djed Spence	12.6m
Andy Robertson	12.5m
Ola Aina	12.5m
Rival players consistently got frustrated having to deal with the teenage talent in 2024/25.

Finding it hard to snatch the ball away from him, Lewis-Skelly was fouled 3.09 times per 90 minutes in his debut campaign.

Only two Premier League players won more free-kicks on average.

In so many ways, the youngster provides added benefits to Arsenal’s starting XI.

Most fouls won per 90 mins 24/25*
Player	Fouls won/90
Jordan Ayew	3.19
James Maddison	3.17
Myles Lewis-Skelly	3.09
Bruno Guimaraes	2.96
John McGinn	2.78
*Minimum 15 starts

Highlight so far
During an unbelievable 2025 for Lewis-Skelly, featuring a string of outstanding performances, his first Arsenal goal stands out as the highlight at club level.

Dancing into the box before firing a powerful shot into the corner, his strike against Man City in a 5-1 win – and subsequent celebration – made headline news.

He is sure to score plenty more for the Gunners.

A grounded young star
Lewis-Skelly’s future is likely to be inside Arsenal’s midfield, and we could see him used there more often in 2025/26.

His composure on the ball, neat passing, physicality and ball-carrying skills make him ideally suited to operate in either the No 6 or No 8 roles he imagined himself playing in.

For now though, he seems very happy to develop his game as a left-back and there will be no complaints from him about playing in that position.

Lewis-Skelly is grounded and has been studying Spanish and Business Studies at AS Level in his spare time.

He also supports his mum, Marcia, with the No 1 Fan Club she launched in 2022 - an initiative aimed at providing support, education and a community for parents whose children are involved at football academies.

On and off the pitch, Lewis-Skelly is a talented, likeable individual with an exceptionally bright future ahead of him.

<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }

            "type": "holdEvents"
        });
    }
</script>

<script>
    // Check if cookie wall is accepted sufficiently
    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well
    window.addEventListener('OneTrustGroupsUpdated', (event) => {
        if (event.detail.includes('C0002')) {
            window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
            window.optimizely.push({
                "type": "sendEvents"
            });
        } else {
            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined
            // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
            window["optimizely"].push({
                "type": "disable",
                "scope": "tracking"
            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

            #onetrust-pc-sdk {
  min-width: 0;
  width: 100% !important;
  max-width: 800px !important;
  top: 50% !important;
  transform: translate(0, -50%);
  border-radius: 4px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

    <article class="article" data-article-id="3533373" data-video-id="" data-widget="article/article" data-initialised="true">
            <header class="article__header">
                <div class="article__header-info">
                    <h1 class="article__header-title">Season trends: Record low number of red cards</h1>

                    <div class="article__tags js-tag-list">
                    </div>
                </div>

                    <div class="article__header-media">
                            <div class="article__header-image">
    
        <div class="image-wrapper">
        <picture class=" picture ">

                    <source class="" srcset="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/4bdff84e-79c3-45bc-95d0-626e20c479b7/Casemiro.jpg?width=768, https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/4bdff84e-79c3-45bc-95d0-626e20c479b7/Casemiro.jpg?width=1536 2x" media="(max-width: 768px)" fetchpriority="high">
                    <source class="" srcset="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/4bdff84e-79c3-45bc-95d0-626e20c479b7/Casemiro.jpg?width=1440, https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/4bdff84e-79c3-45bc-95d0-626e20c479b7/Casemiro.jpg?width=2880 2x" media="(min-width: 769px)" fetchpriority="high">
                    <img class="  picture__img" src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/4bdff84e-79c3-45bc-95d0-626e20c479b7/Casemiro.jpg?width=1440" alt="Casemiro" fetchpriority="high" width="1440" height="960">
        </picture>

                            <span class="article__author">
                                Adrian Clarke
                            </span>

                            <span class="article__separator"></span>

                            <span class="article__publish-date">
                                
        <time class="date " datetime="09 Jun 2023">

            09 Jun 2023
        </time>

                    <div class="article__share-container js-share-container"><button aria-label="Share News" class="button button__icon-only button__icon-right button--small button--filled-tonal article__share-button"><span class="button__icon-wrapper" style="transform: rotate(0deg);"><svg aria-hidden="true" data-testid="svgElement" class="icon "><title data-testid="svgTitle">Share News</title><use xmlnsXlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" href="/resources/v1.44.5/i/svg-output/icons.svg#icn-share"></use></svg></span></button></div>

            </header>

        <div class="article__wrapper">
                <p class="article__subtitle">Talking Tactics</p>

                <p class="article__summary">Adrian Clarke looks at the underlying reasons sendings-off are less than half than the total from eight years ago</p>

            <div class="article__content js-article__content" data-flatplan-body="">
                <p>Only 17 straight red cards were issued in 2022/23 - the fewest number in Premier League history.</p>
<p>Raising the threshold for fouls and serious foul play, top-flight referees showed a little more leniency.</p>
<p>This was reflected in the number of times both teams finished a match with a full complement of players.</p>
<p>Taking into consideration dismissals for two yellow card offences, this season’s total of 30 red cards was the fewest since 1993/94's total of 28.</p>
<p>Across the previous four seasons, an average of 45.5 players were sent off per campaign, so this is a pronounced drop.</p>
<p>In 2014/15, only eight years ago, the tally stood at 71.</p>
<h6>Red cards in last five seasons</h6>
<div class="article__table article__table-scrollable"><table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 91%; height: 57.6776px;" border="0">
<thead>
<tr style="height: 14.5455px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.5455px;" scope="col">Season</th>
<th style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.5455px;">Red cards</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr style="height: 14.9503px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.9503px;">2018/19</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.9503px;">47</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">2019/20</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">44</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="width: 46.1171%;">2020/21</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%;">48</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="width: 46.1171%;">2021/22</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%;">43</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="width: 46.1171%;">2022/23</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%;">30</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
<p>At the end of the 2021/22 season all 20 Premier League clubs had registered at least one sending off, but this year six sides went the distance without losing a player.</p>
<p>There were no red cards for players at <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/127/Bournemouth/overview">AFC Bournemouth</a>, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/1/Arsenal/overview">Arsenal</a>, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/131/Brighton-and-Hove-Albion/overview">Brighton & Hove Albion</a>, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/15/Nottingham-Forest/overview">Nottingham Forest</a>, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/20/Southampton/overview">Southampton</a> and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/25/West-Ham-United/overview">West Ham United</a>.</p>
<p>Across the board we saw high levels of self-control from players, in addition to a degree of tolerance from the match officials.</p>
<p>Overall, 1,374 yellow cards were issued (the second highest on record) but only 0.96 per cent of those first cautions led to a second yellow later on in matches.</p>
<h6>Wolves had too much bite</h6>
<p><a href="https://www.premierleague.com/players/5793/Casemiro/stats">Casemiro</a> grabbed the headlines by being the only individual player to receive two red cards in 2022/23, but as a team it was <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/clubs/38/Wolverhampton-Wanderers/overview">Wolverhampton Wanderers</a> who had the worst disciplinary record.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">



    <figure class="embeddable-photo ">
        <picture class=" picture ">
                    <source class="" srcset="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=600&height=350, https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=1200&height=700 2x, https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=1800&height=1050 3x" media="(max-width: 400px)">
                    <source class="" srcset="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=900&height=500, https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=1800&height=1000 2x, https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=2700&height=1500 3x" media="(max-width: 839px)">
                    <img loading="lazy" class="article__image picture__img" src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2023/06/09/d9e9e229-3188-4cfc-88dc-c7f1cae8a542/Diego-Costa-Wolves-red-card.jpg?width=1400&height=800" alt="Diego Costa, Wolves red card">
        </picture>
            <figcaption class="embeddable-photo__description">
                Diego Costa was one of six Wolves players sent off in 2022/23
            </figcaption>
    </figure>
</div><p>They received six red cards last season, twice the number of any other side in the competition.</p>
<p>Remarkably, only one of those dismissals was for two yellow card offences.</p>
<p>In simple terms, Wolves were responsible for 29.4 per cent of all red cards last season!</p>
<h6>Most red cards 2022/23</h6>
<div class="article__table article__table-scrollable"><table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 91%; height: 85.8594px;" border="0">
<thead>
<tr style="height: 14.5455px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.5455px;" scope="col">Team</th>
<th style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.5455px;">Red cards</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr style="height: 14.9503px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.9503px;">Wolves</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.9503px;">6</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Spurs</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">3</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Chelsea</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">3</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Leeds</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">3</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Crystal Palace</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">3</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
<p>Of the 17 straight reds shown by referees last season, almost half of those were issued due to serious foul play.</p>
<h6>Red card offences 2022/23</h6>
<div class="article__table article__table-scrollable"><table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 91%; height: 85.8594px;" border="0">
<thead>
<tr style="height: 14.5455px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.5455px;" scope="col">Offence</th>
<th style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.5455px;">Red cards</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr style="height: 14.9503px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.9503px;">Serious foul play</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.9503px;">8</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Violent conduct</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">5</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Professional foul</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Handball</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">1</td>
</tr>
<tr style="height: 14.0909px;">
<th style="width: 46.1171%; height: 14.0909px;">Foul language</th>
<td style="width: 53.7438%; height: 14.0909px;">1</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div>
<p>How much did VAR play a part?</p>
<p>The use of VAR contributed to only an additional three red cards across the season.</p>
<p>Officials at Stockley Park rarely intervened with the on-pitch referee’s initial decisions, influencing nine key red card decisions across the 380 matches.</p>
<p>Three red cards were downgraded to yellow cards on review, while six players were sent off for incidents picked up via the video technology.</p>
<h6>Red cards issued after VAR intervention 22/23</h6>

gold_text:
Talking Tactics

Adrian Clarke looks at the underlying reasons sendings-off are less than half than the total from eight years ago

Only 17 straight red cards were issued in 2022/23 - the fewest number in Premier League history.

Raising the threshold for fouls and serious foul play, top-flight referees showed a little more leniency.

This was reflected in the number of times both teams finished a match with a full complement of players.

Taking into consideration dismissals for two yellow card offences, this season’s total of 30 red cards was the fewest since 1993/94's total of 28.

Across the previous four seasons, an average of 45.5 players were sent off per campaign, so this is a pronounced drop.

In 2014/15, only eight years ago, the tally stood at 71.
Red cards in last five seasons
Season	Red cards
2018/19	47
2019/20	44
2020/21	48
2021/22	43
2022/23	30
At the end of the 2021/22 season all 20 Premier League clubs had registered at least one sending off, but this year six sides went the distance without losing a player.

There were no red cards for players at AFC Bournemouth, Arsenal, Brighton & Hove Albion, Nottingham Forest, Southampton and West Ham United.

Across the board we saw high levels of self-control from players, in addition to a degree of tolerance from the match officials.

Overall, 1,374 yellow cards were issued (the second highest on record) but only 0.96 per cent of those first cautions led to a second yellow later on in matches.

Wolves had too much bite
Casemiro grabbed the headlines by being the only individual player to receive two red cards in 2022/23, but as a team it was Wolverhampton Wanderers who had the worst disciplinary record.
Diego Costa was one of six Wolves players sent off in 2022/23
They received six red cards last season, twice the number of any other side in the competition.

Remarkably, only one of those dismissals was for two yellow card offences.

In simple terms, Wolves were responsible for 29.4 per cent of all red cards last season!

Most red cards 2022/23
Team	Red cards
Wolves	6
Spurs	3
Chelsea	3
Leeds	3
Crystal Palace	3
Of the 17 straight reds shown by referees last season, almost half of those were issued due to serious foul play.

Red card offences 2022/23
Offence	Red cards
Serious foul play	8
Violent conduct	5
Professional foul	2
Handball	1
Foul language	1
How much did VAR play a part?

The use of VAR contributed to only an additional three red cards across the season.

Officials at Stockley Park rarely intervened with the on-pitch referee’s initial decisions, influencing nine key red card decisions across the 380 matches.

Three red cards were downgraded to yellow cards on review, while six players were sent off for incidents picked up via the video technology.

Red cards issued after VAR intervention 22/23
Player	Match
Diego Costa	Brentford v WOLVES
Nathaniel Chalobah	FULHAM v Newcastle
Douglas Luiz	Fulham v ASTON VILLA
Casemiro	MAN UTD v Crystal Palace
Casemiro	MAN UTD v Southampton
Jonny	WOLVES v Leeds
Red cards rescinded after VAR intervention 22/23
Player	Match
Gabriel	Leeds v ARSENAL
Kieran Trippier	NEWCASTLE v Man City
Hakim Ziyech	Spurs v CHELSEA

<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }


    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') == -1){

        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "holdEvents"
        });
    }
</script>

<script>
    // Check if cookie wall is accepted sufficiently
    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well
    window.addEventListener('OneTrustGroupsUpdated', (event) => {
        if (event.detail.includes('C0002')) {
            window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
            window.optimizely.push({
                "type": "sendEvents"
            });
        } else {
            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined
            // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
            window["optimizely"].push({
                "type": "disable",
                "scope": "tracking"
            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

            #onetrust-pc-sdk {
  min-width: 0;
  width: 100% !important;
  max-width: 800px !important;
  top: 50% !important;
  transform: translate(0, -50%);
  border-radius: 4px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }


    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') == -1){
        // Hold events if cookie wall is not accepted and no decision was made yet
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "holdEvents"
        });
    }
</script>

<script src="https://cdn.optimizely.com/js/5530288147660800.js"></script>

<script>
    // Check if cookie wall is accepted sufficiently
    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well
    window.addEventListener('OneTrustGroupsUpdated', (event) => {
        if (event.detail.includes('C0002')) {
            window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
            window.optimizely.push({
                "type": "sendEvents"
            });
        } else {
            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined
            // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
            window["optimizely"].push({
                "type": "disable",
                "scope": "tracking"
            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

  min-width: 0;
  width: 100% !important;
  max-width: 800px !important;
  top: 50% !important;
  transform: translate(0, -50%);
  border-radius: 4px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }

    // Check if cookie wall is accepted sufficiently
    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well
    window.addEventListener('OneTrustGroupsUpdated', (event) => {
        if (event.detail.includes('C0002')) {
            window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
            window.optimizely.push({
                "type": "sendEvents"
            });
        } else {
            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined
            // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
            window["optimizely"].push({
                "type": "disable",
                "scope": "tracking"
            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

            #onetrust-pc-sdk {
  min-width: 0;
  width: 100% !important;
  max-width: 800px !important;
  top: 50% !important;
  transform: translate(0, -50%);
  border-radius: 4px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

        <time class="date " datetime="21 Apr 2026">

            21 Apr 2026
        </time>

                            </span>

                    <div class="article__share-container js-share-container"><button aria-label="Share News" class="button button__icon-only button__icon-right button--small button--filled-tonal article__share-button"><span class="button__icon-wrapper" style="transform: rotate(0deg);"><svg aria-hidden="true" data-testid="svgElement" class="icon "><title data-testid="svgTitle">Share News</title><use xmlnsXlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" href="/resources/v1.44.5/i/svg-output/icons.svg#icn-share"></use></svg></span></button></div>
