        <div class="article__wrapper">

                <p class="article__summary">Our daily round-up of the latest news from clubs and players across the Premier League</p>

            <div class="article__content js-article__content" data-flatplan-body="">
                <p><em>Here is your daily briefing for the latest Premier League stories on Tuesday 21 April.</em></p>
<h5>Stats show City's title charge can survive Rodri absence</h5>
<p>Pep Guardiola has revealed that <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/players/220566/rodri/overview">Rodri</a> is out of <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/43/manchester-city/overview">Manchester City</a>'s crucial Premier League game against <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/90/burnley/overview">Burnley</a> – but how big a blow will losing the Ballon d’Or winner be?</p>
<p>Rodri has been one of the game’s leading defensive midfielders during his seven years at the Etihad Stadium and his influence on Guardiola's team is clear in the numbers.</p>
<p>According to Opta, Man City have won 140 of the 195 games he has played — a win rate of 71.8 per cent. In the 65 games he has missed, they have won 39, dropping the win percentage to 60.</p>
<p>With Rodri in the side, they score slightly more goals — averaging 2.4 per game compared to 2.1 — and concede slightly fewer: 0.8 versus 1.1.</p>
<p>Man City are in a groove right now, winning three straight games against <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/14/liverpool/overview">Liverpool</a>, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/8/chelsea/overview">Chelsea</a> and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/3/arsenal/overview">Arsenal</a> with an unchanged starting outfield 10.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4637644" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="Rodri ruled out for Man City at Burnley Content Card" href="https://www.mancity.com/news/mens/burnley-v-manchester-city-team-news-and-tactics-1-63912475" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback "><div class="content-card__image"><div class="js-lazy-load u-observed lazy-image-wrapper " style=""><picture class="media-thumbnail__image object-fit-cover-picture"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2026/04/22/4ae8b0cc-7fac-4d0c-ae31-26e830720df5/Rodri-puts-his-hands-on-his-head-in-disappointment.jpg?height=186&width=332" alt="Rodri puts his hands on his head in disappointment" loading="lazy" class="img undefined picture__img object-fit-cover-picture__img" width="332" height="186"></picture></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">Rodri ruled out for Man City at Burnley</p></div><div class="content-card__details"><span data-testid="contentCardlabelTag" class="content-card__priority-tag-label">Club News</span></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><p>You might then expect Rodri's influence this season to follow the same pattern. But the statistics tell a different story.</p>
<p>Rodri has played 20 league games this season and City have won only half of them. During his two-month injury absence between November and January, they won 10 of 12 league matches – an impressive 83.3 per cent.</p>
<p>They have conceded the same average goals per game — 0.9 — but have scored more than one goal per game more without him in the side.</p>
<p>Guardiola did not rule out Rodri returning for the remainder of The Run In. "We’ll see for the next games — the FA Cup semi-final against Southampton [on Saturday] or 12 days later for the Premier League game at <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/11/everton/overview">Everton</a>," he said.</p>
<h5><span style="color: black;">Wenger makes Arsenal title prediction</span></h5>
<p>If anyone understands Arsenal and winning Premier League titles, it is Arsene Wenger.</p>
<p>The former manager won the trophy three times with Arsenal, including the club's most recent triumph in 2004.</p>
<p>He has now backed his former club to get over the line this season as they enter the final stages of a tense title race with Man City.</p>
<p>Arsenal were nine points clear less than two weeks ago, but after Man City’s win at the Etihad on Sunday, Guardiola’s side have the chance to go top on goal difference with a win against Burnley on Wednesday evening.</p>
<p>Man City's remaining fixtures are, on paper, tougher than Arsenal's as four of their five games after they face Burnley are against top-half opponents.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4572119" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="Premier League title race: How it stands and remaining fixtures Content Card" href="//www.premierleague.com/en/news/4572119/premier-league-title-race-how-it-stands-and-remaining-fixtures" target="_self" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback "><div class="content-card__image"><div class="js-lazy-load u-observed lazy-image-wrapper " style=""><picture class="media-thumbnail__image object-fit-cover-picture"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2026/04/20/2710118a-e2cc-4aca-bce9-c385fb53021f/Collage-of-Pep-Guardiola-with-Erling-Haaland-and-Mikel-Arteta-clapping-Arsenal-fans-with-The-Run-In-title-overlay.png?height=186&width=332" alt="Collage of Pep Guardiola with Erling Haaland and Mikel Arteta clapping Arsenal fans with The Run In title overlay" loading="lazy" class="img undefined picture__img object-fit-cover-picture__img" width="332" height="186"></picture></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">Premier League title race: How it stands and remaining fixtures</p></div><div class="content-card__details"><span data-testid="contentCardlabelTag" class="content-card__priority-tag-label">News</span></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><p>"I believe that Arsenal will win the league," Wenger said at an Abbey Road Studios fundraiser for <a href="https://www.twinningproject.org/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">The Twinning Project</a>.</p>
<p>"I deeply believe. It looks, to me, like common sense. I like Eze, Odegaard, Havertz and Madueke's attacking [intent at the Etihad]. Manchester City will not make the perfect run-in."</p>
<p>The BBC’s <em>Monday Night Club</em> radio programme also posed an interesting debate: whether Arsenal’s final run-in mirrors Rory McIlroy’s 2025 Masters playoff win against Justin Rose.</p>
<p>When McIlroy lost a four-stroke lead and it went to a playoff, his caddie Harry Diamond said: "We would have taken this on Monday morning."</p>
<p>Would Arsenal have taken being level on points with Man City with five games to play at the start of the season?</p>
<h5>Bournemouth’s new Rose</h5>
<p><a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/91/bournemouth/overview">AFC Bournemouth</a>'s outgoing manager Andoni Iraola believes the club "will be in very good hands" when Marco Rose replaces him in the summer.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.afcb.co.uk/news/2026/april/20/marco-rose-to-become-head-coach-ahead-of-26-27-season/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Bournemouth confirmed on Monday the German coach will join</a> when Iraola steps down at the end of the season.</p>
<p>Iraola will be a hard act to follow, but Rose shares many of his traits. He is credited with helping develop Jude Bellingham and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/players/223094/erling-haaland/overview">Erling Haaland</a> at Borussia Dortmund and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/players/424876/dominik-szoboszlai/overview">Dominik Szoboszlai</a> at RB Leipzig.</p>
<p>Rose also guided Borussia Monchengladbach to Champions League qualification on a limited budget.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4637071" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="Marco Rose: How Jurgen Klopp influenced Bournemouth's new head coach Content Card" href="//www.premierleague.com/en/news/4637071/marco-rose-how-jurgen-klopp-influenced-bournemouths-new-head-coach" target="_self" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback "><div class="content-card__image"><div class="js-lazy-load u-observed lazy-image-wrapper " style=""><picture class="media-thumbnail__image object-fit-cover-picture"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2026/04/20/3320da26-4250-4e90-9005-ee03350dad71/Jurgen-Klopp-and-Marco-Rose.jpg?height=186&width=332" alt="Jurgen Klopp and Marco Rose" loading="lazy" class="img undefined picture__img object-fit-cover-picture__img"></picture></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">Marco Rose: How Jurgen Klopp influenced Bournemouth's new head coach</p></div><div class="content-card__details"><span data-testid="contentCardlabelTag" class="content-card__priority-tag-label">Features</span></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><p>"He's a great manager," Iraola said. "I praised him a few years ago, I always liked his teams and I hope he continues that.</p>
<p>"They should be happy because he's someone who can definitely improve the team. I'm sure the players are happy they know what's happening."</p>
<p>Iraola can leave his successor the gift of European football if Bournemouth maintain their current form. They are on the Premier League's longest unbeaten run this season after winning or drawing their last 13 matches.</p>
<p>A draw or win against <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/2/leeds-united/overview">Leeds United</a> on Wednesday will move them into sixth place.</p>
<p>However, <span style="color: black;">Iraola cautioned: "It's going to be so difficult for us because we are competing [for a European place] against not one or two but a lot of teams."</span></p>
<h5>'Best couple of weeks' ever for Leeds</h5>
<p>Daniel Farke has described the current period as "one of the best couple of weeks in Leeds' modern history”.</p>
<p>Leeds had been sliding towards the relegation battle involving <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/17/nottingham-forest/overview">Nottingham Forest</a>, <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/21/west-ham-united/overview">West Ham United</a> and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/6/tottenham-hotspur/overview">Tottenham Hotspur</a>.</p>
<p>But back-to-back victories against <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/1/manchester-united/overview">Manchester United</a> and <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/39/wolverhampton-wanderers/overview">Wolverhampton Wanderers</a> have eased them away from the bottom three. Victory against Bournemouth would take them to 42 points and all but guarantee safety.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4637649" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="Farke provides Leeds fitness update ahead of Bournemouth match Content Card" href="https://www.leedsunited.com/en/news/daniel-farke-provides-fitness-update-ahead-of-bournemouth-clash" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback "><div class="content-card__image"><div class="js-lazy-load u-observed lazy-image-wrapper " style=""><picture class="media-thumbnail__image object-fit-cover-picture"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2026/04/21/d41952c8-2226-4bda-a757-53ad0f1d6aa8/Farke-and-Stach.png?height=186&width=332" alt="Farke and Stach" loading="lazy" class="img undefined picture__img object-fit-cover-picture__img"></picture></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">Farke provides Leeds fitness update ahead of Bournemouth match</p></div><div class="content-card__details"><span data-testid="contentCardlabelTag" class="content-card__priority-tag-label">Club News</span></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><p>Following that, Leeds face Chelsea in an FA Cup semi-final at Wembley on Sunday.</p>
<p>While Farke is enjoying the moment, he will not allow his players to ease off.</p>
<p>"We have achieved a lot but nothing is done yet," the Leeds manager said. "We want and need more points, so we will stay with the foot on the gas.</p>
<p>"It's one battle after the other. We concentrate always on the next game and we have five opportunities to win more points. That's the mindset we go with and we are highly motivated to win as many points as possible in a difficult away game against Bournemouth."</p>
<h5>Wolves fans 'deserve better'</h5>
<p>Relegation had been looming for Wolves for several months, but this made it no less painful when their fate was confirmed by West Ham's draw with <a href="https://www.premierleague.com/en/clubs/31/crystal-palace/overview">Crystal Palace</a> on Monday night.</p><div class="articleWidget full-width">
<div data-d2c="" class="embeddable-article" data-widget="article/embeddable-article" data-article-reference="4632497" data-initialised="true">
<div class="embeddable-article__container"><article data-testid="contentCard" data-analytics-link-location="Content Card" class="content-card content-card--horizontal content-card--article"><a aria-label="Wolves relegated to the Championship after West Ham draw at Palace Content Card" href="//www.premierleague.com/en/news/4632497/wolves-relegated-to-the-championship-after-west-ham-draw-at-palace" target="_self" class="content-card__button js-button"></a><div data-testid="contentCardThumbnail" class="content-card__thumbnail content-card__thumbnail--large content-card__thumbnail--fallback "><div class="content-card__image"><div class="js-lazy-load u-observed lazy-image-wrapper " style=""><picture class="media-thumbnail__image object-fit-cover-picture"><img src="https://resources.premierleague.pulselive.com/photo-resources/2026/04/18/43005ea7-d4c7-47c7-833c-60e1e84ac2df/An-image-of-a-Wolves-player-and-head-coach-Rob-Edwards.jpg?height=186&width=332" alt="An image of a Wolves player and head coach Rob Edwards" loading="lazy" class="img undefined picture__img object-fit-cover-picture__img"></picture></div></div></div><div class="content-card__info"><div class="content-card__title-container"><p data-testid="contentCardTitle" class="content-card__title">Wolves relegated to the Championship after West Ham draw at Palace</p></div><div class="content-card__details"><span data-testid="contentCardlabelTag" class="content-card__priority-tag-label">News</span></div></div></article></div></div>
</div><p>Nathan Shi has been Wolves' interim executive chairman since December and, with relegation likely then, started planning for life outside the Premier League.</p>
<p>"You deserve better, and giving you a club you can genuinely be proud of is what drives everything we do from this point forward," <a href="https://www.wolves.co.uk/news/club/20260420-a-message-from-nathan-shi-following-relegation/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Shi told the club's website</a>. </p>
<p>Wolves were last relegated from the Premier League in the 2011/12 season. Demotion to League One came the following year, but they came straight back up from that division as champions with 103 points. Four seasons in the Championship later, they returned to the top flight.</p>
<p>They have since enjoyed eight consecutive seasons in the Premier League, including two seventh-placed finishes.</p>
<p>"Confirmation of our relegation is a difficult moment for everyone connected to Wolves," Shi said. "While this is a deeply disappointing outcome, work has been under way since my arrival in December to ensure we are ready to respond with clarity and conviction.</p>
<p>"We are clear on what needs to improve, and our focus is now on strengthening the club, building momentum, and creating a team our supporters can believe in. We know what is required and will approach the months ahead with purpose."</p>
            </div>
        </div>
    </article>

    </div>

gold_text:
Our daily round-up of the latest news from clubs and players across the Premier League

Here is your daily briefing for the latest Premier League stories on Tuesday 21 April.

Stats show City's title charge can survive Rodri absence
Pep Guardiola has revealed that Rodri is out of Manchester City's crucial Premier League game against Burnley – but how big a blow will losing the Ballon d’Or winner be?

Rodri has been one of the game’s leading defensive midfielders during his seven years at the Etihad Stadium and his influence on Guardiola's team is clear in the numbers.

According to Opta, Man City have won 140 of the 195 games he has played — a win rate of 71.8 per cent. In the 65 games he has missed, they have won 39, dropping the win percentage to 60.

With Rodri in the side, they score slightly more goals — averaging 2.4 per game compared to 2.1 — and concede slightly fewer: 0.8 versus 1.1.

Man City are in a groove right now, winning three straight games against Liverpool, Chelsea and Arsenal with an unchanged starting outfield 10.

You might then expect Rodri's influence this season to follow the same pattern. But the statistics tell a different story.

Rodri has played 20 league games this season and City have won only half of them. During his two-month injury absence between November and January, they won 10 of 12 league matches – an impressive 83.3 per cent.

They have conceded the same average goals per game — 0.9 — but have scored more than one goal per game more without him in the side.

Guardiola did not rule out Rodri returning for the remainder of The Run In. "We’ll see for the next games — the FA Cup semi-final against Southampton [on Saturday] or 12 days later for the Premier League game at Everton," he said.

Wenger makes Arsenal title prediction
If anyone understands Arsenal and winning Premier League titles, it is Arsene Wenger.

The former manager won the trophy three times with Arsenal, including the club's most recent triumph in 2004.

He has now backed his former club to get over the line this season as they enter the final stages of a tense title race with Man City.

Arsenal were nine points clear less than two weeks ago, but after Man City’s win at the Etihad on Sunday, Guardiola’s side have the chance to go top on goal difference with a win against Burnley on Wednesday evening.

Man City's remaining fixtures are, on paper, tougher than Arsenal's as four of their five games after they face Burnley are against top-half opponents.

"I believe that Arsenal will win the league," Wenger said at an Abbey Road Studios fundraiser for The Twinning Project.

"I deeply believe. It looks, to me, like common sense. I like Eze, Odegaard, Havertz and Madueke's attacking [intent at the Etihad]. Manchester City will not make the perfect run-in."

The BBC’s Monday Night Club radio programme also posed an interesting debate: whether Arsenal’s final run-in mirrors Rory McIlroy’s 2025 Masters playoff win against Justin Rose.

When McIlroy lost a four-stroke lead and it went to a playoff, his caddie Harry Diamond said: "We would have taken this on Monday morning."

Would Arsenal have taken being level on points with Man City with five games to play at the start of the season?

Bournemouth’s new Rose
AFC Bournemouth's outgoing manager Andoni Iraola believes the club "will be in very good hands" when Marco Rose replaces him in the summer.

Bournemouth confirmed on Monday the German coach will join when Iraola steps down at the end of the season.

Iraola will be a hard act to follow, but Rose shares many of his traits. He is credited with helping develop Jude Bellingham and Erling Haaland at Borussia Dortmund and Dominik Szoboszlai at RB Leipzig.

Rose also guided Borussia Monchengladbach to Champions League qualification on a limited budget.

"He's a great manager," Iraola said. "I praised him a few years ago, I always liked his teams and I hope he continues that.

"They should be happy because he's someone who can definitely improve the team. I'm sure the players are happy they know what's happening."

Iraola can leave his successor the gift of European football if Bournemouth maintain their current form. They are on the Premier League's longest unbeaten run this season after winning or drawing their last 13 matches.

A draw or win against Leeds United on Wednesday will move them into sixth place.

However, Iraola cautioned: "It's going to be so difficult for us because we are competing [for a European place] against not one or two but a lot of teams."

'Best couple of weeks' ever for Leeds
Daniel Farke has described the current period as "one of the best couple of weeks in Leeds' modern history”.

Leeds had been sliding towards the relegation battle involving Nottingham Forest, West Ham United and Tottenham Hotspur.

But back-to-back victories against Manchester United and Wolverhampton Wanderers have eased them away from the bottom three. Victory against Bournemouth would take them to 42 points and all but guarantee safety.
Following that, Leeds face Chelsea in an FA Cup semi-final at Wembley on Sunday.

While Farke is enjoying the moment, he will not allow his players to ease off.

"We have achieved a lot but nothing is done yet," the Leeds manager said. "We want and need more points, so we will stay with the foot on the gas.

"It's one battle after the other. We concentrate always on the next game and we have five opportunities to win more points. That's the mindset we go with and we are highly motivated to win as many points as possible in a difficult away game against Bournemouth."

Wolves fans 'deserve better'
Relegation had been looming for Wolves for several months, but this made it no less painful when their fate was confirmed by West Ham's draw with Crystal Palace on Monday night.
Nathan Shi has been Wolves' interim executive chairman since December and, with relegation likely then, started planning for life outside the Premier League.

"You deserve better, and giving you a club you can genuinely be proud of is what drives everything we do from this point forward," Shi told the club's website. 

Wolves were last relegated from the Premier League in the 2011/12 season. Demotion to League One came the following year, but they came straight back up from that division as champions with 103 points. Four seasons in the Championship later, they returned to the top flight.

They have since enjoyed eight consecutive seasons in the Premier League, including two seventh-placed finishes.

"Confirmation of our relegation is a difficult moment for everyone connected to Wolves," Shi said. "While this is a deeply disappointing outcome, work has been under way since my arrival in December to ensure we are ready to respond with clarity and conviction.

"We are clear on what needs to improve, and our focus is now on strengthening the club, building momentum, and creating a team our supporters can believe in. We know what is required and will approach the months ahead with purpose."

    function getCookie(name) {

        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {

            }

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;

<section data-widget="single-match-page/single-match-page" data-match-id="2562225" data-page-content="overview" data-project-id="0ece2d8f-52d8-4b3d-a409-36bac21f2280" data-use-sso="false" data-custom-route-page="man-of-the-match" data-initialised="true">
<div data-testid="singleMatchPageTabs" class="single-match-page__tabs"><div class="wireframe-ui"><div class="tabs tabs--primary"><div class="base-Tabs-root base-Tabs-horizontal"><div tabindex="-1" role="tablist" class="base-TabsList-root base-TabsList-horizontal"><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/overview" class="base-Tab-root base--selected">Recap</a><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/highlights" class="base-Tab-root ">Highlights</a><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/lineups" class="base-Tab-root ">Lineups</a><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/stats" class="base-Tab-root ">Stats</a><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/table" class="base-Tab-root ">Table</a><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/commentary" class="base-Tab-root ">Commentary</a><a href="/en/match/2562225/bournemouth-vs-leeds-united/info" class="base-Tab-root ">Match info</a></div><div class="single-match-page__side-by-side"><div class="match-report-wrapper"><button data-testid="matchReportInternal" aria-label="Match Report" class="match-report match-report--button"><article><header class="match-report__title">Report<div class="match-report__icon"><svg aria-hidden="true" data-testid="svgElement" class="icon "><title data-testid="svgTitle">Open match report</title><use xmlnsXlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" href="/resources/v1.44.5/i/svg-output/icons.svg#icn-expand"></use></svg></div></header><p class="match-report__summary"><div class="wrapper col-12">
	<div class="standardArticle js-article__content">
	    <p class="p1">Sean Longstaff scored an equaliser in the SEVENTH minute of stoppage time as Leeds United moved nine points clear of the bottom three with a 2-2 draw at AFC Bournemouth. </p>
<p class="p1">Rayan thought he had secured three points for the Cherries in the 85th minute, but substitute Longstaff had other ideas as he lashed past goalkeeper Djordje Petrovic in the dying seconds. </p>
<p class="p1">Junior Kroupi opened the scoring on the hour mark after 'keeper Karl Darlow kept things level heading into the break, but a James Hill own goal saw Leeds draw level soon after. </p>
<p class="p1">Second-half substitute Rayan swept in from Tyler Adams' cross late on, only for another Leeds long throw to cause havoc and lead to an equaliser moments after Evanilson had a goal disallowed for offside. </p>
<p class="p1">Bournemouth missed the chance to go sixth, but now sit one point behind Brighton & Hove Albion, while Leeds, unbeaten in five Premier League matches, remain 15th. </p>
<p class="p1"><strong>How the match unfolded</strong></p>
<p>In a fairly quiet first half, Bournemouth's Evanilson flashed an acrobatic attempt across the face of goal early on before Darlow made a stunning save with his foot to keep out Ryan Christie.</p>
<p>Kroupi broke the deadlock after his strike was deflected past Darlow by the sliding Joe Rodon following a fantastic tackle and pass from Marcos Senesi.</p>
<p>Leeds almost hit back immediately through the in-form Noah Okafor, but his dinked attempt struck the post; however, they were level in after 68 minutes when Wilfried Gnonto's drilled cross was turned in by Cherries defender Hill.</p>
<p>Two Bournemouth substitutes combined with five minutes to go as Adams' low centre was steered home by Rayan, but the drama was far from over.</p>
<p>Evanilson saw a goal disallowed before Lukas Nmecha volleyed against the post at the other end, though it mattered little for the German as Longstaff sent the away end into raptures with a fine strike after the hosts failed to clear their lines from another long throw.</p>
    
        <div class="u-hide" data-script="pl_article-table" data-widget="article-table"></div>
	</div>
</div>

gold_text:
Bournemouth
2
-
2
HT 0 - 0
Leeds
Report
Sean Longstaff scored an equaliser in the SEVENTH minute of stoppage time as Leeds United moved nine points clear of the bottom three with a 2-2 draw at AFC Bournemouth. 

Rayan thought he had secured three points for the Cherries in the 85th minute, but substitute Longstaff had other ideas as he lashed past goalkeeper Djordje Petrovic in the dying seconds. 

Junior Kroupi opened the scoring on the hour mark after 'keeper Karl Darlow kept things level heading into the break, but a James Hill own goal saw Leeds draw level soon after. 

Second-half substitute Rayan swept in from Tyler Adams' cross late on, only for another Leeds long throw to cause havoc and lead to an equaliser moments after Evanilson had a goal disallowed for offside. 

Bournemouth missed the chance to go sixth, but now sit one point behind Brighton & Hove Albion, while Leeds, unbeaten in five Premier League matches, remain 15th. 

How the match unfolded

In a fairly quiet first half, Bournemouth's Evanilson flashed an acrobatic attempt across the face of goal early on before Darlow made a stunning save with his foot to keep out Ryan Christie.

Kroupi broke the deadlock after his strike was deflected past Darlow by the sliding Joe Rodon following a fantastic tackle and pass from Marcos Senesi.

Leeds almost hit back immediately through the in-form Noah Okafor, but his dinked attempt struck the post; however, they were level in after 68 minutes when Wilfried Gnonto's drilled cross was turned in by Cherries defender Hill.

Two Bournemouth substitutes combined with five minutes to go as Adams' low centre was steered home by Rayan, but the drama was far from over.

Evanilson saw a goal disallowed before Lukas Nmecha volleyed against the post at the other end, though it mattered little for the German as Longstaff sent the away end into raptures with a fine strike after the hosts failed to clear their lines from another long throw.

<script>
  // Make sure this is run before the Optimizely script is executed so it stops events from being sent
  // You can also move this logic to the Project JavaScript within Optimizely itself
  // IMPORTANT: Write your own getCookie function if you need to read out OneTrust or another tool
    function getCookie(name) {
        var nameEQ = name + "=";
        var cookies = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
            var c = cookies[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) === ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) {
                return decodeURIComponent(c.substring(nameEQ.length));
            }
        }
        return "";
    }


    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') == -1){

        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "holdEvents"
        });
    }
</script>

<script>
    // Check if cookie wall is accepted sufficiently
    if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:0') != -1 && getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('interactionCount=0') == -1) {
        // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
        window["optimizely"].push({
            "type": "disable",
            "scope": "tracking"
        });
    } else if (getCookie('OptanonConsent').indexOf('C0002:1') != -1) {
        // Send events once cookie wall is accepted
        window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
        window.optimizely.push({
            "type": "sendEvents"
        });
    }
        
    // OPTIONAL: Fire it on interaction with the cookie banner as well
    window.addEventListener('OneTrustGroupsUpdated', (event) => {
        if (event.detail.includes('C0002')) {
            window.optimizely = window.optimizely || [];
            window.optimizely.push({
                "type": "sendEvents"
            });
        } else {
            // Check if cookie wall was explicitely declined
            // Opt out visitors once cookie wall is declined
            window["optimizely"].push({
                "type": "disable",
                "scope": "tracking"
            }); 
        }
    });
</script>

            #onetrust-pc-sdk {
  min-width: 0;
  width: 100% !important;
  max-width: 800px !important;
  top: 50% !important;
  transform: translate(0, -50%);
  border-radius: 4px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-header {
   min-height: 34px;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
padding: 20px 0 20px 30px;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-pc-logo {
  margin-left: 4px;
  height: 34px !important;
  width: auto !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-content {
   top: 75px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk #ot-pc-desc .privacy-notice-link {
      display: none;
}

.ot-pc-footer-logo {
    display: none;
}
    
.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::before {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

.otPcTab [class*='btn-']::after {
    background: none;
    cursor: auto;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk {
    border-radius: 4px;
}

.ot-acc-hdr {
   padding: 12px 0 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item:first-of-type {
   border: none !important;
}
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-accordion-layout.ot-cat-item {
    border: none;
    border-top: 2px solid #e7e9eb;
}
  
.ot-btn-container button:before, 
.ot-btn-container button:after, 
.ot-btn-subcntr:before,
.ot-btn-subcntr:after {
    display: none;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk button {
    border-radius: 4px !important;
    font-size: 14px !important;
}
    
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob:before {
    background-color: #37003c;
}
    
.ot-cat-header,
.ot-pvcy-hdr {
    font-size: 16px !important;
}
    
.ot-category-desc,
.ot-pc-desc {
    font-size: 14px !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #E8E8E8 !important;
    border: 1px solid #E8E8E8 !important;
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-switch-nob:before {
background-color: #fff !important;
box-shadow: 0px 3px 8px rgb(0 0 0 / 15%), 0px 3px 1px rgb(0 0 0 / 6%);
}

#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl input:checked+.ot-switch .ot-switch-nob {
    background-color: #00FF87 !important;
    border: 1px solid #fff !important;
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
    #onetrust-pc-sdk {
       border-radius: 4px !important;
    }

    .ot-cat-header,
    .ot-pvcy-hdr {
        font-size: 14px !important;
    }
        
    .ot-category-desc,
    .ot-pc-desc {
        font-size: 12px !important;
    }

    #onetrust-pc-sdk button {
        line-height: 1 !important;
        padding: 10px !important;
    }

<section data-widget="single-match-page/single-match-page" data-match-id="2562223" data-page-content="overview" data-project-id="0ece2d8f-52d8-4b3d-a409-36bac21f2280" data-use-sso="false" data-custom-route-page="man-of-the-match" data-initialised="true">
<div data-testid="singleMatchPageTabs" class="single-match-page__tabs"><div class="wireframe-ui"><div class="tabs tabs--primary"><div class="base-Tabs-root base-Tabs-horizontal"><div tabindex="-1" role="tablist" class="base-TabsList-root base-TabsList-horizontal"><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/overview" class="base-Tab-root base--selected">Recap</a><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/highlights" class="base-Tab-root ">Highlights</a><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/lineups" class="base-Tab-root ">Lineups</a><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/stats" class="base-Tab-root ">Stats</a><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/table" class="base-Tab-root ">Table</a><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/commentary" class="base-Tab-root ">Commentary</a><a href="/en/match/2562223/nottingham-forest-vs-burnley/info" class="base-Tab-root ">Match info</a></div><div class="single-match-page__side-by-side"><div class="match-report-wrapper"><button data-testid="matchReportInternal" aria-label="Match Report" class="match-report match-report--button"><article><header class="match-report__title">Report<div class="match-report__icon"><svg aria-hidden="true" data-testid="svgElement" class="icon "><title data-testid="svgTitle">Open match report</title><use xmlnsXlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" href="/resources/v1.44.5/i/svg-output/icons.svg#icn-expand"></use></svg></div></header><p class="match-report__summary"><div class="wrapper col-12">
	<div class="standardArticle js-article__content">
	    <p>Morgan Gibbs-White’s hat-trick boosted Nottingham Forest’s hopes of staying up as they recovered from a goal down to thrash Burnley 4-1 and move five points clear of the bottom three.</p>
<p>The England international recorded his first treble for Forest, scoring three times in the space of 15 second-half minutes in an impressive comeback win at the City Ground.</p>
<p>Burnley went ahead in the second minute of first-half added time, with striker Zian Flemming netting his ninth Premier League goal of the campaign.</p>
<p>However, Gibbs-White took centre stage with a trio of superb finishes to round off a brilliant week, having also scored the winner in Thursday’s UEFA Europa League victory over Porto.</p>
<p>Igor Jesus netted in the eighth minute of stoppage time to add further gloss to an emphatic victory for 16th-place Forest, while 19th-place Burnley remain firmly in the relegation zone.</p>
<p><strong>How the match unfolded</strong></p>
<p>A quiet first half almost came to life in the 25th minute, as Forest goalkeeper Matz Sels got down well to push away Burnley midfielder James Ward-Prowse's dipping free-kick.<br><br>But the visitors punished Forest’s flat display in first-half stoppage time, as Flemming swept past Sels following a patient build-up.<br><br>The hosts improved after the break. Gibbs-White failed to connect with a volley, but the Forest captain made no mistake in the 62nd minute, slotting home after former Forest man Ward-Prowse failed to cut out a ball back into the Burnley box.<br><br>Gibbs-White then completed the turnaround on 69 minutes, thumping in a volley from team-mate Omari Hutchinson's cross.<br><br>The playmaker then made it a hat-trick eight minutes later, sending a looping header from Ryan Yates’ centre beyond Burnley ‘keeper Martin Dubravka's despairing dive.<br><br>Substitute Jesus raced away to add a fourth in stoppage time, as the hosts took a big step towards securing their Premier League status.</p>
<p><strong>Club reports</strong></p>
<p><a href="https://www.nottinghamforest.co.uk/news/2026/april/19/gibbs-white-s-hat-trick-helps-forest-to-three-points-against-burnley/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Nottingham Forest report</a> | <a href="https://burnleyfootballclub.com/content/match-report-forest-0-0-burnley" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Burnley report</a></p>

	</div>
</div>

gold_text:
Nott'm Forest
4
-
1
HT 0 - 1
Burnley
Report
Morgan Gibbs-White’s hat-trick boosted Nottingham Forest’s hopes of staying up as they recovered from a goal down to thrash Burnley 4-1 and move five points clear of the bottom three.

The England international recorded his first treble for Forest, scoring three times in the space of 15 second-half minutes in an impressive comeback win at the City Ground.

Burnley went ahead in the second minute of first-half added time, with striker Zian Flemming netting his ninth Premier League goal of the campaign.

However, Gibbs-White took centre stage with a trio of superb finishes to round off a brilliant week, having also scored the winner in Thursday’s UEFA Europa League victory over Porto.

Igor Jesus netted in the eighth minute of stoppage time to add further gloss to an emphatic victory for 16th-place Forest, while 19th-place Burnley remain firmly in the relegation zone.

How the match unfolded

A quiet first half almost came to life in the 25th minute, as Forest goalkeeper Matz Sels got down well to push away Burnley midfielder James Ward-Prowse's dipping free-kick.

But the visitors punished Forest’s flat display in first-half stoppage time, as Flemming swept past Sels following a patient build-up.

The hosts improved after the break. Gibbs-White failed to connect with a volley, but the Forest captain made no mistake in the 62nd minute, slotting home after former Forest man Ward-Prowse failed to cut out a ball back into the Burnley box.

Gibbs-White then completed the turnaround on 69 minutes, thumping in a volley from team-mate Omari Hutchinson's cross.

The playmaker then made it a hat-trick eight minutes later, sending a looping header from Ryan Yates’ centre beyond Burnley ‘keeper Martin Dubravka's despairing dive.

Substitute Jesus raced away to add a fourth in stoppage time, as the hosts took a big step towards securing their Premier League status.

    The Briefing: Welbeck's Ronaldo tribute, Pogba praises Mainoo and more

    David Coverdale 22 Apr 2026

    Our daily round-up of the latest news from clubs and players across the Premier League

    Here is your daily briefing for the latest Premier League stories on Wednesday 22 April.

    Welbeck’s Ronaldo tribute – and how he keeps getting better

    Danny Welbeck paid homage to his former Manchester United team-mate Cristiano Ronaldo after equalling the Brighton & Hove Albion record for the most goals in a single Premier League season.

    The 35-year-old striker came off the bench to score the Seagulls’ third goal in their 3-0 win over Chelsea on Tuesday night, marking his strike by copying Ronaldo’s iconic "Siu" celebration.

    Watch: Welbeck's goal and 'Siu' celebration against Chelsea

    Danny Welbeck 🤝 Cristiano Ronaldo@OfficialBHAFC pic.twitter.com/hU4kDSdk3x

    — Premier League (@premierleague) April 22, 2026

    Welbeck made his Man Utd debut in Ronaldo’s last season of his first stint with Man Utd in 2008/09.

    The England international has previously spoken of sitting next to Ronaldo in the dressing room and being inspired by his dedication in training.

    Special night under the Amex lights ✨
    Another W, let’s keep this momentum going 🙌🏿
    SIUUUU @Cristiano #BHAFC
    +3 pic.twitter.com/bbnwMqHAPU

    — Danny Welbeck (@DannyWelbeck) April 21, 2026

    Welbeck has now scored 13 top-flight goals this season, matching Glenn Murray’s club-record tally set in 2018/19.

    It is also the most prolific season of his career, bettering his 10 league goals from 2024/25, and he is now the outright top English scorer in the division in 2025/26, moving one clear of Nottingham Forest's Morgan Gibbs-White.

    Welbeck’s form has led to calls for him to be selected in England’s squad for this summer’s FIFA World Cup. The most recent of his 42 caps came in 2018.

    “I think he should have a chance of going to the World Cup,” former Brighton midfielder Steve Sidwell told Sky Sports. “He’s in fine form and that’s what you want going into a tournament.”

    Guardiola declares 'love' for Rice message

    Pep Guardiola has expressed his admiration for Declan Rice following the Arsenal midfielder’s defiant message after last Sunday’s title showdown.

    Rice was caught on camera declaring “it’s not done” to his captain Martin Odegaard, moments after the Gunners lost 2-1 to rivals Manchester City at the Etihad Stadium.

    Asked about that viral clip, Man City boss Guardiola told Sky Sports: “I love that. That’s why Arsenal is there.

    “It shows what Declan Rice is, what the Arsenal mentality is. We've faced it in the Premier League these past seasons and in the Carabao Cup, how competitive they are.

    “Otherwise, they can't have done the season they have done, or the seasons they have done before [without having that mentality]."

    Watch: Guardiola responds to viral Rice moment

    "I love that."

    Pep Guardiola was impressed by Declan Rice's reaction after Arsenal's defeat to Manchester City 🤝 pic.twitter.com/j8nskhWla9

    — Sky Sports Premier League (@SkySportsPL) April 22, 2026

    He added: “If people think because we beat them, the title race is done, Declan Rice showed us that they are there.”

    Man City are three points behind Premier League leaders Arsenal, but play their game in hand at Burnley on Wednesday night.

    Arteta’s son rising up the ranks at Arsenal

    Gabriel Arteta, the eldest son of Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta, has taken another step forward in his Gunners career after featuring for their Under-18s in a competitive match for the first time.

    The 16-year-old winger came on as a substitute during the U18 Premier League South match at Reading on Wednesday.

    Arteta junior's rise raises the prospect of him becoming the latest son to play under their father in the Premier League.

    Mikel Arteta with his son Gabriel (centre) in May 2023
    Loading...

    Alex Bruce played for his dad Steve Bruce at both Birmingham City and Hull City, Jamie Redknapp was Harry Redknapp’s first signing at Southampton, and Gavin Strachan featured for his father Gordon Strachan at Coventry City.

    Nigel Clough was Nottingham Forest’s top scorer for Brian Clough when they were relegated in the inaugural Premier League campaign in 1992/93, while Darren Ferguson claimed a title winner’s medal under Sir Alex Ferguson at Man Utd in the same season.

    In January this year, twin brothers Jack Fletcher and Tyler Fletcher were on the bench when their dad Darren Fletcher was in caretaker charge of Man Utd, but they did not get on, later making their respective debuts under Michael Carrick.

    Other near misses include Maurizio Pochettino, who joined the Southampton academy when his father Mauricio Pochettino took charge, then followed him to Tottenham Hotspur, but did not play in the first team.

    Tom Ince has played in the Premier League and been managed by his dad Paul Ince, but not at the same time.

    Pogba praises Mainoo

    Former Man Utd midfielder Paul Pogba has backed Kobbie Mainoo to have a “great career” at Old Trafford.

    England international Mainoo has impressed for Man Utd since Carrick took charge, having struggled for game time under former head coach Ruben Amorim.

    Speaking on Rio Ferdinand Presents, Pogba said of the 21-year-old: “He’s such a talented kid.

    “It's really hard for me to say he should leave when he's so loved by the people at United. He's such a great player, [has] big potential and he can have a great career at Manchester United.

    “I just wish him the best. I don't know what decision he's going to take. I just want to see this kid play because it's enjoyable to see him play.”

    Pogba, who made 157 Premier League appearances for Man Utd over two spells, also praised captain Bruno Fernandes, who has provided 18 assists in the top flight this season – just two shy of Kevin De Bruyne and Thierry Henry’s single-season Premier League record.

    “He can do everything,” added Pogba. “He's everywhere on the pitch.

    “He has a volume. He keeps running. He's smart. He can play two touches, one touch. He can shoot. Football lovers will always respect Bruno Fernandes.”

    Coventry confirmed as Championship title winners

    Four days after sealing promotion to the Premier League, Coventry City secured the Championship title with an emphatic 5-1 home win over Portsmouth on Tuesday night.

    “It was very apt for these boys to do it in that style at the CBS,” Sky Blues boss Frank Lampard, a Premier League Hall of Fame member, told BBC CWR.

    “I was very determined for the players to do it in the right way. I thought it was fantastic and it was perfect as an evening.”

    Frank Lampard has done an incredible job at Coventry 👏 pic.twitter.com/BgbujYhona

    — Premier League (@premierleague) April 22, 2026

    Coventry have now scored 90 goals in the Championship this season, amassing 89 points from 44 games.

    When Lampard took charge of the club in November 2024, they were just two points clear of the relegation zone in 17th place.

    Related Content
